# Team News: Haiti vs. Scotland injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Oliver Thomas| 13 Jun 2026 02:00

© Imago / Pro Sports Images

Scotland kick-start their 2026 World Cup campaign with a Group C fixture against Caribbean island Haiti at Boston Stadium in Massachusetts on Sunday.

These two nations are joined in their group by five-time world champions Brazil and African giants Morocco, and here, Sports Mole rounds up all the latest team news for the two nations.

---

## HAITI vs. SCOTLAND

Haiti vs. Scotland World Cup 2026 Match Preview ⚽ | "You Heard It Here First"

## HAITI

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Placide; Arcus, Ade, Delcroix, Experience; Deedson, Jean Jacques, Bellegarde, Providence; Isidor, Nazon

## SCOTLAND

Out: Billy Gilmour(knee)

Doubtful: Scott McTominay(stomach), Sc

# Haiti vs. Scotland projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group C game in Boston

Dom Farrell

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Haiti and Scotland each end a long absence from the biggest stage when they face one another in their World Cup Group C opener.

On their only previous tournament appearance, Haiti lost all three matches in 1974 and bowed out at the group stage.

Scotland also fell in the round robin stage in 1974, as they have done on all eight of their World Cup adventures, up to and including their previous outing in 1998.

Victory for Steve Clarke's side would give them a good chance of making history and reaching the knockout rounds.

Defeat would be deeply problematic for either side. Brazil and Morocco round out Group C and are both ranked inside the world's top 10 by FIFA.

2026 WORLD CUP HQ: Latest World Cup news| Full World Cup schedule| Bu

# Scotland vs Haiti injury news: Steve Clarke mulls over major decision on trio as World Cup campaign begins

Sports Writer

Published 11th Jun 2026, 13:38 BST

Here is the latest team news ahead of Scotland v Haiti at the World Cup 2026 at the Gillette Stadium this weekend

Scotland will get their World Cup campaign underway this weekend as they take on Haiti in Group C at the Gillette Stadium in Foxborough on Sunday (kick-off: 2am).

Their pre-tournament preparations now done and dusted, Steve Clarke’s men head into the tournament on the back of two impressive victories, where they downed Curacao 4-1 before thrashing Bolivia 4-0 in New Jersey last weekend.

Set to face Morocco and Brazil in their other group fixtures, the national team are expected to be backed by hordes of Tartan Army supporters as they announce their arrival back on the world stage for the first time since 1998.

Their opponents, Haiti, are making history of their own too, with the Caribbean nation taking part in just their second World Cup, having only previously

# Scotland vs Haiti injury news: Steve Clarke mulls over major decision on trio as World Cup campaign begins

Sports Writer

Published 11th Jun 2026, 13:38 BST

Here is the latest team news ahead of Scotland v Haiti at the World Cup 2026 at the Gillette Stadium this weekend

Scotland will get their World Cup campaign underway this weekend as they take on Haiti in Group C at the Gillette Stadium in Foxborough on Sunday (kick-off: 2am).

Their pre-tournament preparations now done and dusted, Steve Clarke’s men head into the tournament on the back of two impressive victories, where they downed Curacao 4-1 before thrashing Bolivia 4-0 in New Jersey last weekend.

Set to face Morocco and Brazil in their other group fixtures, the national team are expected to be backed by hordes of Tartan Army supporters as they announce their arrival back on the world stage for the first time since 1998.

Their opponents, Haiti, are making history of their own too, with the Caribbean nation taking part in just their second World Cup, having only previously

news_items.title: Haiti vs Scotland at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN
news_items.author: Anirudh Menon
news_items.text:
Haiti vs Scotland at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

A first World Cup in 28 years for Scotland. A first ever for Haiti. As the two underdogs in group C go head-to-head, expect fireworks as they eye a positive start to historic World Cup campaigns. Steve Clarke's Scotland are ranked 40 places above Haiti (who are WR 83) and consequentially start as strong favourites. They come into the World Cup on the back of strong results against Curacao (4-1 W) and Bolivia(4-0) win and their confidence will be high.

Sebastian Migne's Haiti, meanwhile, overcame steep odds to make it to the World Cup in the first place and will not be cowed on the biggest stage of them all. They have had a mixed bag in the World Cup warmups: a 4-0 thrashing of New Zealand was followed by a 2-1 loss to Peru(a game in which they took the lead and held onto until the 81st minute), and they'll be quietly confident of pulling off an upset on debut.

Here's everything you need to know about the match.

## How to watch:

The match will be available on BBC One in the UK, Fox in the U.S., Zee5 in India

news_items.title: Scotland team v Haiti: Predicted team for the World Cup opener - including roles for Hearts and Hibs players
news_items.text:
Scotland team v Haiti: Predicted team for the World Cup opener - including roles for Hearts and Hibs players

News you can trust since 1873

Sign In

BREAKING

# Scotland team v Haiti: Predicted team for the World Cup opener - including roles for Hearts and Hibs players

Sports journalist

Published 9th Jun 2026, 12:00 BST

Craig Gordon and Grant Hanley are in the squad - but will they play?

Scotland’s first World Cup finals game for 28 years is just days away and national coach Steve Clarke is finalising his team selection. He has 26 players to pick from and will inevitably leave many disappointed when he names the starting line-up to face Haiti in Foxborough in the early hours of Sunday morning.

Clarke is expected to start with a 4-4-2 formation and must decide which striking pair to go with. In goal, Hearts keeper Craig Gordon is fit again but Nottingham Forest’s Angus Gunn has generally been Scotland’s No.1 whenever available. Gordon is expected to begin this World Cup watching from the sidelines with Gunn on the pitch, but Hibs defender Grant Hanley is likely to be handed a more prominent role.

Our predicted Scotland team to face Haiti is below:

## 1. GK: Angus Gunn

SNS G

# Team News: Haiti vs. Scotland injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Oliver Thomas| 13 Jun 2026 02:00

© Imago / Pro Sports Images

Scotland kick-start their 2026 World Cup campaign with a Group C fixture against Caribbean island Haiti at Boston Stadium in Massachusetts on Sunday.

These two nations are joined in their group by five-time world champions Brazil and African giants Morocco, and here, Sports Mole rounds up all the latest team news for the two nations.

---

## HAITI vs. SCOTLAND

Haiti vs. Scotland World Cup 2026 Match Preview ⚽ | "You Heard It Here First"

## HAITI

Out: None

Doubtful: None

## SCOTLAND

# Haiti vs. Scotland projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group C game in Boston

Dom Farrell

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Haiti and Scotland each end a long absence from the biggest stage when they face one another in their World Cup Group C opener.

On their only previous tournament appearance, Haiti lost all three matches in 1974 and bowed out at the group stage.

Scotland also fell in the round robin stage in 1974, as they have done on all eight of their World Cup adventures, up to and including their previous outing in 1998.

Victory for Steve Clarke's side would give them a good chance of making history and reaching the knockout rounds.

Defeat would be deeply problematic for either side. Brazil and Morocco round out Group C and are both ranked inside the world's top 10 by FIFA.

# Scotland vs Haiti injury news: Steve Clarke mulls over major decision on trio as World Cup campaign begins

Sports Writer

Published 11th Jun 2026, 13:38 BST

Here is the latest team news ahead of Scotland v Haiti at the World Cup 2026 at the Gillette Stadium this weekend

Scotland will get their World Cup campaign underway this weekend as they take on Haiti in Group C at the Gillette Stadium in Foxborough on Sunday (kick-off: 2am).

Their pre-tournament preparations now done and dusted, Steve Clarke’s men head into the tournament on the back of two impressive victories, where they downed Curacao 4-1 before thrashing Bolivia 4-0 in New Jersey last weekend.

Set to face Morocco and Brazil in their other group fixtures, the national team are expected to be backed by hordes of Tartan Army supporters as they announce their arrival back on the world stage for the first time since 1998.

Their opponents, Haiti, are making history of their own too, with the Caribbean nation taking part in just their second World Cup, having only previously"}
{"title": "Haiti vs Scotland at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN", "url": "https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49026945/haiti-scotland-kickoff-how-watch-stats-team-news-fifa-world-cup-2026", "publishedDate": "2026-06-11T09:02:00.000Z", "author": "Anirudh Menon", "text": "Haiti vs Scotland at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

A first World Cup in 28 years for Scotland. A first ever for Haiti. As the two underdogs in group C go head-to-head, expect fireworks as they eye a positive start to historic World Cup campaigns. Steve Clarke's Scotland are ranked 40 places above Haiti (who are WR 83) and consequentially start as strong favourites. They come into the World Cup on the back of strong results against Curacao (4-1 W) and Bolivia(4-0) win and their confidence will be high.

Sebastian Migne's Haiti, meanwhile, overcame steep odds to make it to the World Cup in the first place and will not be cowed on the biggest stage of them all. They have had a mixed bag in the World Cup warmups: a 4-0 thrashing of New Zealand was followed by a 2-1 loss to Peru(a game in which they took the lead and held onto until the 81st minute), and they'll be quietly confident of pulling off an upset on debut.

Here's everything you need to know about the match.

## How to watch:

The match will be available on BBC One in the UK, Fox in the U.S., Zee5 in India"}

# Scotland team v Haiti: Predicted team for the World Cup opener - including roles for Hearts and Hibs players

Sports journalist

Published 9th Jun 2026, 12:00 BST

Craig Gordon and Grant Hanley are in the squad - but will they play?

Scotland’s first World Cup finals game for 28 years is just days away and national coach Steve Clarke is finalising his team selection. He has 26 players to pick from and will inevitably leave many disappointed when he names the starting line-up to face Haiti in Foxborough in the early hours of Sunday morning.

Clarke is expected to start with a 4-4-2 formation and must decide which striking pair to go with. In goal, Hearts keeper Craig Gordon is fit again but Nottingham Forest’s Angus Gunn has generally been Scotland’s No.1 whenever available. Gordon is expected to begin this World Cup watching from the sidelines with Gunn on the pitch, but Hibs defender Grant Hanley is likely to be handed a more prominent role.

Our predicted Scotland team to face Haiti is below:

## 1. GK: Angus Gunn
